title: Linearization Gives A 1D Chiral Mode
summary: Linearizing the dispersion around a one-dimensional Fermi crossing and replacing momentum deviation by -i d/dx yields a continuum chiral Hamiltonian whose sign of velocity fixes the branch direction.
assumptions: The crossing is simple with nonzero slope.
scope: Lecture One derivation from local band data to chiral continuum field theory.

source_anchors.notes: This is the first continuum reduction in Lecture One.

representation: Low-energy derivation from Taylor expansion plus momentum-to-derivative substitution.
failure_modes: A single chiral branch is only a local low-energy description, not a global lattice theory.
retrieval_hints: Use when a query asks how a band crossing becomes a relativistic chiral mode.
